turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: Good morning, Kingsbridge Public Library, Olivia speaking. How can I help you?

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Hi there. I'd like to join the library, please. I've recently moved to the area and I'd love to start borrowing some books.

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: Wonderful, I can register you right now over the phone and you can pick up your card later. Let me just take a few details. Could I start with your surname?

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Yes, it's Fielding.

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: Could you spell that for me, please?

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Of course. It's F-I-E-L-D-I-N-G. Fielding. And my first name is Marcus.

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: Thank you, Marcus. And what's your address?

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: It's forty-two Bristol Road — oh, sorry, no, I always do that. It's Bridge Road. Forty-two Bridge Road.

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: No problem at all — forty-two Bridge Road. And could I take the postcode too?

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: It's K-T-six, four-N-P. So that's KT6 4NP.

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: Lovely. And what's the best contact number for you?

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: My mobile is oh-seven-eight-one-two, three-three-five, nine-four-six.

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: Let me read that back — oh-seven-eight-one-two, three-three-five, nine-four-six. Is that right?

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: That's correct, yes.

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: Perfect. Now, there's no charge to join, but I do need you to bring one document as proof of your address. A recent utility bill or a bank statement is fine. A driving licence on its own, I'm afraid, won't be accepted for the address.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: That's fine — I can bring a recent electricity bill.

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: That's ideal. Now, as a standard member you can borrow up to twelve items at any one time, and the loan period is three weeks. You can renew online twice if you need a little longer.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: And what happens if I bring something back late?

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: There's a small fine of twenty pence per item per day, up to a maximum of five pounds. Your membership card will be ready to collect from the front desk, and your membership number will be printed on the back.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Great. One last thing — what time do you close on Saturdays?

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: During the week we're open until seven, but on Saturdays we close a bit earlier, at five o'clock. And we're shut all day on Sundays.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: That's really helpful. And can I reserve books online, or do I have to come in each time?

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: Oh, you can do everything online once you're set up — search the catalogue, reserve books from any of our branches, and even borrow e-books and audiobooks through our app. When a reserved book comes in, we'll send you an email to let you know it's ready to collect.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: That's brilliant, exactly what I was hoping for. Is there parking nearby if I drive over?

turns.speaker: Olivia
turns.text: There's a small car park round the back, and it's free for the first two hours, which is plenty of time to browse. Just bring your ticket to the desk and we'll stamp it for you.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Perfect. Thanks so much for your help, Olivia.
